comment_text.sn4.17:1.2: Continuing the theme of Māra and the senses.

root_text.sn4.17:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.sn4.17:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ upādāya bhikkhūnaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.sn4.17:1.3: Te ca bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.sn4.17:2.2: “ayaṁ kho samaṇo gotamo channaṁ phassāyatanānaṁ upādāya bhikkhūnaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sappahaṁseti.
root_text.sn4.17:2.3: Te ca bhikkhū aṭṭhiṁ katvā manasi katvā sabbacetasā samannāharitvā ohitasotā dhammaṁ suṇanti.
root_text.sn4.17:2.4: Yannūnāhaṁ yena samaṇo gotamo tenupasaṅkameyyaṁ vicakkhukammāyā”ti.
root_text.sn4.17:2.5: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato avidūre mahantaṁ bhayabheravaṁ saddamakāsi, apissudaṁ pathavī maññe undrīyati. Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn4.17:2.7: Evaṁ vutte, bhagavā taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn4.17:2.9: Māro eso pāpimā tumhākaṁ vicakkhukammāya āgato”ti.
root_text.sn4.17:2.10: Atha kho bhagavā “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:

translation_text.sn4.17:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.sn4.17:1.2: Now at that time the Buddha was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk on the topic of the six fields of contact.
translation_text.sn4.17:1.3: And those mendicants were paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.sn4.17:2.2: “This ascetic Gotama is educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the mendicants with a Dhamma talk on the topic of the six fields of contact.
translation_text.sn4.17:2.3: And those mendicants are paying attention, applying the mind, concentrating wholeheartedly, and actively listening.
translation_text.sn4.17:2.4: Why don’t I go and pull the wool over their eyes?”
translation_text.sn4.17:2.5: Then Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and made a terrifyingly loud noise close by him. It seemed as if the earth were shattering, so that one of the mendicants said to another,
translation_text.sn4.17:2.6: “Mendicant, mendicant, it seems like the earth is shattering!”
translation_text.sn4.17:2.7: When this was said, the Buddha said to that mendicant,
translation_text.sn4.17:2.9: That’s Māra the Wicked come to pull the wool over your eyes!”
translation_text.sn4.17:2.10: Then the Buddha, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.17:3.2: touches, and ideas, the lot of them—
translation_text.sn4.17:4.1: But a mindful disciple of the Buddha
translation_text.sn4.17:4.3: Having slipped free of Māra’s dominion,

variant_text.sn4.17:2.5: undrīyati → udrīyati (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
